sections.paragraph:
ἀβαναύσως adv. of uncertain mng.; not in narrow-minded fashion, unselfishly, nobly prob. do justice to the term; λειτουργεῖν ἀ. beside ἀμέμπτως, μετὰ ταπεινοφροσύνης and ἡσύχως 1 Cl 44:3. The adj. ἀβάναυσος stands betw. εὔσπλαγχνος and ἀγαπητικός Const. Apost. 2, 3, 3 and is understood by the Syrian of the Didasc. Apost. Syr. (p. 35 Lewis) as תָנַן kind; cp. Clem. Al., Paed. 3, 34 φιλανθρώπως, οὐ βαναύσως.—DELG. s.v. βάναυσος.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

adj. adj. = adjective

betw. betw. = between

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Clem. Al. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1
